name: Tour of the Xúquer Riverbank — Circular from Alzira
name_original: Tour por la Ribera del Xúquer — Circular desde Alzira
region_intro: No traffic road cycling routes around Río Júcar traverse a diverse landscape, from dramatic canyons carved by the river to lush riverbanks and fertile agricultural plains. The region features significant elevation changes in areas with towering limestone cliffs, particularly around Cuenca and the Júcar River Canyon. As the river approaches the coast, the terrain transitions to flatter sections through orange groves, offering varied gradients for road cyclists. This topography provides a range of cycling experiences across its natural features. Best no traffic road cycling routes around Río Júcar The most popular no traffic road cycling route is Finca Tío Ger loop from Alzira, an easy 24.9 miles (40.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 44 minutes to complete, featuring gentle gradients through agricultural areas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Views from the Tous Pass – Tous Dam loop from Alzira, a moderate 44.9 miles (72.2 km) path. This route offers significant ascents and panoramic views over the Tous Dam and surrounding canyons. Local road cyclists also love the Ascent to Port de Barx loop from Alzira, a 49.9 miles (80.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain with moderate climbs, often completed in about 3 hours 49 minutes. Road cycling around Río Júcar is defined by dramatic canyons, lush riverbanks, and fertile agricultural plains, offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Río Júcar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 1,800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Río Júcar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic road cycling routes are available in the Río Júcar region?
A: There are over 50 dedicated no-traffic road cycling routes around Río Júcar. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 10 easy, 42 moderate, and 1 difficult option to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on no-traffic road cycling routes in Río Júcar?
A: The Río Júcar region offers a diverse range of terrain for no-traffic road cycling. You'll find routes winding through lush riverbanks and fertile agricultural plains, particularly as the river approaches the coast. Further inland, especially around areas like Cuenca and the Júcar River Canyon, routes feature more dramatic canyons and mountainous sections with significant elevation changes and breathtaking views.

Q: Are there any easy, no-traffic road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 10 easy, no-traffic road cycling routes in the Río Júcar region. These typically follow flatter sections along riverbanks or through agricultural plains, offering gentle gradients. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39541484" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Finca Tío Ger loop from Alzira&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 40 km ride.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I can see along the no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: Along your no-traffic road cycling routes, you can encounter several interesting landmarks. Keep an eye out for structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4489746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Raconada Iron Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3331792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alzira Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. You might also cycle near natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3229055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Levee&lt;/a&gt; or scenic paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4846081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path Through the Southern Júcar Marshes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging no-traffic road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: While most routes are easy to moderate, the region does offer options with more significant climbs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Tous Pass – Tous Dam loop from Alzira&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 72 km route with over 760 meters of elevation gain, providing a good challenge. The region's canyons also offer opportunities for more demanding ascents.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the no-traffic routes in Río Júcar?
A: The no-traffic road cycling routes in Río Júcar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from serene riverbanks to dramatic canyons, and the opportunity to explore charming villages and natural wonders away from vehicle traffic.

Q: Are there any circular no-traffic road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the no-traffic road cycling routes in Río Júcar are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39541484" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Finca Tío Ger loop from Alzira&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39546557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Port de Barx loop from Alzira&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for your ride.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through charming villages or historical sites?
A: Absolutely. The Río Júcar region is dotted with charming villages and historical sites that many routes pass near or through. Alcalá del Júcar, known as one of Spain's prettiest villages with houses carved into rock, and towns like Jalance and Jorquera, with their medieval roots, offer cultural richness alongside the natural scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year to go no-traffic road cycling in Río Júcar?
A: The Río Júcar region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, especially in inland areas, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to cycle year-round, though some higher elevation routes might be cooler.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Júcar River Canyon or other significant natural features?
A: Yes, the Júcar River Canyon is a prominent natural feature, and routes in areas like Cuenca and between Jalance and Cofrentes will offer spectacular views of its towering limestone cliffs and deep ravines. The region's diverse topography ensures many routes provide impressive natural backdrops, including the lush riverbanks and the source of the Júcar River in the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, which features waterfalls and crystal-clear waters.
tips: Drinking water fountain in a very pleasant environment with trees, ideal for cooling off in the summer. A few meters away there is a bar and recreational area
Spectacular. The Valencian Alpe d'Huez with its horseshoes. A classic in the area.
